sourceMovie.title: The Incredible Hulk
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Incredible Hulk works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of The Incredible Hulk is alive in Guardians of the Galaxy (2014), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Endgame
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Avengers: Endgame (2019) operates in the same science-fiction as The Incredible Hulk, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Black Panther
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Black Panther (2018) belongs to the same conversation as The Incredible Hulk — both anchor their science-fiction in action emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Incredible Hulk's science-fiction stayed with you, Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 (2017) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The action through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Captain America: The First Avenger
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Incredible Hulk excels in science-fiction, Captain America: The First Avenger (2011) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spider-Man 2 (2004) is the natural next step after The Incredible Hulk: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Star Trek
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Incredible Hulk who respond to its science-fiction will find Star Trek (2009) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Incredible Hulk fans keep landing on Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse (2023) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Jurassic Park III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Jurassic Park III draws from the same science-fiction well as The Incredible Hulk, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2001 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Bloodshot
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Bloodshot (2020) captures the science-fiction appeal of The Incredible Hulk without simply imitating it. The action streak running through both films is what puts Bloodshot at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Incredible Hulk?
faq.answer: Try Guardians of the Galaxy, Avengers: Endgame, Black Panther—each shares DNA with The Incredible Hulk while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Incredible Hulk?
faq.answer: The Incredible Hulk (2008) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Incredible Hulk worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
